answer:
**The work is the *Yājñavalkya Smṛti*, Chapter 3 ("Chapter 3," titled "On Expiation" or *prāyaścittādhyāya* in Sanskrit).**[^1]

This chapter provides the requested anatomical inventory of the human body in verses 84–103, matching the specified numbers precisely: three hundred and sixty bones;[^2] sixty-four teeth (with sockets), twenty nails;[^3] seventy-two ribs (with sockets and nodules);[^4] forty-five [bones] in the back, fifteen in the neck;[^5] seventeen [bones] in the chest;[^6] seven hundred veins, nine hundred sinews, two hundred arteries, and five hundred muscles;[^7] one hundred and seven vital spots and two hundred joints;[^8] and body hairs together with the sweat ducts numbering "fifty crores and four crores ... and sixty-seven and a half lakhs."[^9]

The verse declaring "three hundred and sixty bones" is:  
> His bodies are sixfold, and six skins support them;  
> there are six limbs, and **three hundred and sixty bones**. // Yj_3.84 //[^10]

The verse giving "seven hundred veins, nine hundred sinews, two hundred arteries, and five hundred muscles" is:  
> **Seven hundred veins, nine hundred sinews,**  
> **two hundred arteries, and five hundred muscles.** // Yj_3.100 //[^11]

[^1]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/).

[^4]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-7dcd29).
[^5]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-c7a123).
[^6]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-8c0993).
[^7]: Yājñavalkya Smṛti, [Chapter 3](/works/unknown-yajnavalkya-smrti-cb88d6/03/translation/#p-e42749).
